status: exact positive entire t-compatible modular theta blend with termwise spectral non-promotion guard
proof_boundary: This artifact constructs an exact, strictly positive, entire, even theta-block decomposition of Phi whose blocks remain Schwartz under every bounded positive Newman deformation. It proves normal transform and coupled Laguerre matrix expansions and records explicit negative block-transform and nonreal Jensen witnesses. It does not prove the coupled matrix is positive, strict Laguerre positivity, Lambda<=0, or RH.

theta_summand.scope: The second equality is the theta modular identity.

modular_switch.entire: omega is entire because erf and sinh are entire
modular_switch.erfc_proof: exp(-x^2)-erfc(x)>=0 for x>=0: it starts and ends at zero, and its derivative changes sign once at x=1/sqrt(pi).

blended_blocks.smoothness: b_n is an even entire function

strict_positivity.negative_case: If phi_n(-u)<0 and q=pi*n^2, then q*exp(-4u)<3/2.
strict_positivity.negative_case_threshold: The negative case forces exp(4u)>2*pi/3>2 and hence exp(8u)>4; the displayed slack is then strictly positive.
strict_positivity.conclusion: b_n(u)>0 for every n>=1 and every real u

exact_partition.proof: The sum equals omega(u)*Phi(u)+(1-omega(u))*Phi(-u), then theta modular evenness applies.

positive_time_compatibility.block_tail: For u->+infinity, omega*phi_n(u) is O_n(exp(9u-pi*n^2*exp(4u))) and (1-omega)*phi_n(-u) is O_n(exp(-5u-9*sinh(4u)^2)).
positive_time_compatibility.schwartz: For every finite T, exp(t*u^2)*b_n(u) is Schwartz uniformly for 0<=t<=T.

transform_series.convergence: For every fixed derivative order and finite T, the series is absolutely and uniformly convergent on R_x times [0,T].

coupled_laguerre.convergence: The double series is absolutely and uniformly convergent for x in R and t in every compact positive-time interval.

tail_enclosure.criterion: If L[S_(N,t)] exceeds the displayed error, then L_t(x)>0.
tail_enclosure.scope: The bound is uniform in frequency and uses only positivity of the omitted blended blocks; numerical evaluation of the moments must still be enclosed rigorously before promotion.

moment_floor_guard.positive_floor: For every fixed finite N, mu_(N,j)(T)>0 for j=0,1,2 because the omitted blended tail is strictly positive.
moment_floor_guard.riemann_lebesgue: S_(N,t)(x), S_(N,t)'(x), and S_(N,t)''(x) tend to zero as |x| tends to infinity.
moment_floor_guard.consequence: The moment-only error majorant tends to the positive constant mu_1^2+mu_0*mu_2 while L[S_(N,t)] tends to zero.
moment_floor_guard.decision: For every fixed N, the three-moment criterion is necessarily a compact-frequency certificate and cannot close the global tail.

decaying_tail_enclosure.laguerre_use: Replace each mu_j in the finite-to-infinite Laguerre error by epsilon_j; the same algebraic error inequality remains valid.
decaying_tail_enclosure.finiteness: Every d_(N,j,m)(T) is finite because the blended tail and all of its derivatives remain Schwartz after bounded Newman time.
decaying_tail_enclosure.scope: This repairs the positive moment floor. Rigorous useful constants and a lower bound for the finite partial Laguerre expression remain open.

boundary.decision: The decomposition removes both the cusp obstruction of reflected finite theta truncations and the exponential-tail obstruction of Euler cell blocks. It does not make the block transforms or their mixed Laguerre entries nonnegative.
boundary.live_handoff: Enclose derivative L1 budgets for the modular tail and compare their frequency-decaying Laguerre error with a finite blended partial sum. Termwise Fourier positivity and individual block Laguerre-Polya membership are rejected.

numerics.method: Composite Gauss-Legendre quadrature on [0,4] with analytic Newman weight and the exact modular blend
numerics.jensen_definition: For G_t(z)=B_(1,t)(i*sqrt(z))=sum_(k>=0)gamma_k*z^k/k!, gamma_k=k!*mu_(2k)/(2k)!. The degree-nine shift-zero Jensen polynomial is sum_(j=0)^9 binom(9,j)*gamma_j*X^j.
numerics.scope: Numerical non-promotion witnesses only: positivity of the blended kernel blocks does not make their cosine transforms nonnegative or put the individual transforms in the Laguerre-Polya class.

rows.claim: The entire super-exponential switch exchanges with its complement under reflection.
Every modularly blended theta block is even and entire.
rows.proof_boundary: Block regularity only; no spectral sign follows.

diagnostics.smoothness: b_n is an even entire function

rows.claim: Every modularly blended theta block is strictly positive on the real axis.
rows.formula: b_n(u)>0 for every n>=1 and every real u
rows.proof_boundary: Kernel positivity only; not Fourier positivity.

diagnostics.negative_case: If phi_n(-u)<0 and q=pi*n^2, then q*exp(-4u)<3/2.
diagnostics.negative_case_threshold: The negative case forces exp(4u)>2*pi/3>2 and hence exp(8u)>4; the displayed slack is then strictly positive.
diagnostics.conclusion: b_n(u)>0 for every n>=1 and every real u

rows.claim: The positive blended blocks sum exactly to the Xi Fourier kernel.
rows.proof_boundary: Uses theta modular evenness, not zeta-zero information.

diagnostics.proof: The sum equals omega(u)*Phi(u)+(1-omega(u))*Phi(-u), then theta modular evenness applies.

rows.claim: Every block remains rapidly decreasing after every bounded positive Newman deformation.
rows.formula: For every finite T, exp(t*u^2)*b_n(u) is Schwartz uniformly for 0<=t<=T.

diagnostics.block_tail: For u->+infinity, omega*phi_n(u) is O_n(exp(9u-pi*n^2*exp(4u))) and (1-omega)*phi_n(-u) is O_n(exp(-5u-9*sinh(4u)^2)).
diagnostics.schwartz: For every finite T, exp(t*u^2)*b_n(u) is Schwartz uniformly for 0<=t<=T.

rows.claim: The positive-time block transforms and every fixed spectral jet form a normal series.
rows.proof_boundary: Absolute uniform convergence; no sign of the sum is asserted.

diagnostics.convergence: For every fixed derivative order and finite T, the series is absolutely and uniformly convergent on R_x times [0,T].

rows.claim: The strict-Laguerre target has an absolutely convergent positive-time block matrix expansion.
rows.proof_boundary: The mixed matrix entries retain both signs.

diagnostics.convergence: The double series is absolutely and uniformly convergent for x in R and t in every compact positive-time interval.

rows.claim: Three positive tail moments give a uniform finite-to-infinite Laguerre enclosure.
rows.proof_boundary: Exact conditional enclosure; no finite partial sum is certified globally here.

diagnostics.criterion: If L[S_(N,t)] exceeds the displayed error, then L_t(x)>0.
diagnostics.scope: The bound is uniform in frequency and uses only positivity of the omitted blended blocks; numerical evaluation of the moments must still be enclosed rigorously before promotion.

rows.claim: The positive three-moment enclosure is necessarily compact in frequency for every finite block count.
rows.formula: The moment-only error majorant tends to the positive constant mu_1^2+mu_0*mu_2 while L[S_(N,t)] tends to zero.
rows.proof_boundary: Rejects global promotion of the moment-only enclosure, not the blended decomposition.

diagnostics.positive_floor: For every fixed finite N, mu_(N,j)(T)>0 for j=0,1,2 because the omitted blended tail is strictly positive.
diagnostics.riemann_lebesgue: S_(N,t)(x), S_(N,t)'(x), and S_(N,t)''(x) tend to zero as |x| tends to infinity.
diagnostics.consequence: The moment-only error majorant tends to the positive constant mu_1^2+mu_0*mu_2 while L[S_(N,t)] tends to zero.
diagnostics.decision: For every fixed N, the three-moment criterion is necessarily a compact-frequency certificate and cannot close the global tail.

rows.claim: Derivative L1 budgets give arbitrarily high algebraic decay for every remainder jet.
rows.proof_boundary: Exact tail theorem; no useful global constants or Laguerre lower bound are certified here.

diagnostics.laguerre_use: Replace each mu_j in the finite-to-infinite Laguerre error by epsilon_j; the same algebraic error inequality remains valid.
diagnostics.finiteness: Every d_(N,j,m)(T) is finite because the blended tail and all of its derivatives remain Schwartz after bounded Newman time.
diagnostics.scope: This repairs the positive moment floor. Rigorous useful constants and a lower bound for the finite partial Laguerre expression remain open.

rows.claim: Strict positivity of the blended kernel blocks implies neither nonnegative transforms nor individual Laguerre-Polya membership.
rows.formula: Six block transforms are negative, and the first block's degree-nine Jensen polynomial has a nonreal pair at two times.
rows.proof_boundary: Numerical witnesses only; rejects termwise transform positivity and the naive individual Laguerre-Polya promotion.

diagnostics.method: Composite Gauss-Legendre quadrature on [0,4] with analytic Newman weight and the exact modular blend
diagnostics.jensen_definition: For G_t(z)=B_(1,t)(i*sqrt(z))=sum_(k>=0)gamma_k*z^k/k!, gamma_k=k!*mu_(2k)/(2k)!. The degree-nine shift-zero Jensen polynomial is sum_(j=0)^9 binom(9,j)*gamma_j*X^j.
diagnostics.scope: Numerical non-promotion witnesses only: positivity of the blended kernel blocks does not make their cosine transforms nonnegative or put the individual transforms in the Laguerre-Polya class.

rows.claim: The surviving use of the blend is a coupled spectral coercivity or tail-dominance estimate.
rows.formula: Enclose derivative L1 budgets for the modular tail and compare their frequency-decaying Laguerre error with a finite blended partial sum. Termwise Fourier positivity and individual block Laguerre-Polya membership are rejected.
rows.proof_boundary: Open; not strict Laguerre positivity, Lambda<=0, or RH.
